  { $sort: { "reviews.date": -1 } },
  { $limit: 5 },
  { $project: { _id: 0, comments: "$reviews.comments" } }
])
expected.result:
[{'comments': 'Perfect accommodation, love to stay again'}, {'comments': 'As the many attractions of Australia open, this vast apartment will give you a lifetime experience. The building is next door to the harbour and many exquisite restaurants that will light up your visit! If the hot sun bothers you too much, you can easily go to the pool that also gives you a beautiful view of the city. I recommend this homely apartment to everyone and anyone!'}, {'comments': 'Everything is perfect, I have to say thanks to the host.'}, {'comments': "As you enter this comfortable apartment, it gives you a hearty welcome as you discover the many wonders of Sydney. Other add on's of this visit include a gym if you need a work-out and a wonderful pool that proves a fundamental as part of the torrid atmosphere of the city. The bedrooms are absolutely stunning! All the sheets are clean and the room itself is magnificently spotless. It is located at a perfect spot, with the closeness of the best 5 star hotel! A clear cut stay that's worthwhile!"}, {'comments': 'On the positive side the apartment was in a fabulous location - close to attractions, restaurants and shopping. The check-in was seamless, despite my concerns about picking up keys from a convenience store. The apartment came with parking -  a real bonus in this area of Sydney. It took me 2 turns to find the carpark, but that was my fault I should have watched the video Angela provided on the day rather than a few days before. The apartment was very well equipped - dishwasher, washing machine and dryer etc. The beds were very comfy. Really good Wifi. 
On the negative side I was disappointed that we were only given one bath towel each (for a 4 night stay) and a fairly thin towel at that. No hand towels or face washers. The apartment was clean but could probably do with a spruce up as it looked a little tired. 
My big problem was the TV. It didn't work. When my husband telephoned one of the contact people he received a telephone call back saying that he couldn't help us and would get someone to look at it the next day - a bit too late as we were leaving the next morning. He also told my husband that he had spoken to the Concierge at the building and that a number of apartments were have antenna problems. We then tried to tune  the TV again and were able to "find" over 200 channels - but all of them were locked, including the free to air channels. 
My husband contacted the second contact person and he got back to us and said he couldn't understand why we were having problems as the family that had moved out that morning didn't have any issues. This was a complete lie - we had been in the apartment for the last three nights. 
I don't like people lying to me. Why not simply say that you can't rectify the problem, apologise for the inconvenience and leave it at that.  It wouldn't have been any big deal.
Finally I don't like hosts asking me (on several occasions) to give them a 5 star rating. I only give honest feedback.'}]
